paragraphs.body: Being alone is knowing that you are truly by yourself. No one to hug on another lonely Sunday night. No one to say high to or just to talk to. No one to tell your right from wrong or just tell you to keep going. It is just you! Your mind and thoughts, sometimes those thoughts get deep but that Feeling that you are separated from everyone and everything and you cannot seem to think why you are always alone and do not have anyone. A place where you are in a room filled with people laughing and talking but only echoes of your own thoughts remain and deep inside you are still not feeling that void of being all by yourself. A feeling that feels like you disconnected or feeling unsupported no matter what you do. It is called being alone. Being alone can feel like a heavy silence, it is a void that stretches endlessly it feels like. A scary place to be when you genuinely believe that you have no one so you just isolate yourself. You can use it to benefit you, or you can let it run your life.

place where you are in a room filled with people laughing and talking but only echoes of your own thoughts remain

Let's talk about your essay's central focusing idea and then "map" your essay to see what's really at the heart of it and how you might best develop and support that idea.

paragraphs.body: A teddy bear is soft and cuddly, it is warm with brown fur that feels gentle to the touch. It has eyes that are round, and it gives a little shin. But the shin is starting to go away as the teddy bear gets old. It gives the presence of a friend, a caring friend. It looks cute and comforting, like you can hug it for days. Its arms and legs are floppy, which is perfect for hugging. The whole body is the perfect shape and right size to hold close. Over time the fur becomes a little worn but that only makes it feel more special because it has memories in it. When you hug it there is a soft sweet scent that reminds you of home and safety. When it is just resting on a shelf or resting on your bed it is always there to comfort you, making it a true friend. It also has a small, stitched nose and a wide friendly smile that makes you to be happy. Even though it lost a couple threads, and the fur is a little worn it is still always there when you are ALONE and just need a hug. The bear is a true friend offering love and support whenever you need it or when you are feeling down, or you just need a little warmth. Every time I go home and see the teddy bear; I had for years it reminds me of the memories from my childhood and now that when I am alone it helps me in a way to think about those old memories and saying it will be okay it will all work out. It was 8th grade going into freshman year and to be honest I did not have a lot of friends. I had some but they were only friends that I see at school and not hang out with after school. I was already alone trying to find my place. But then my grandpa got sick, and I had no one. The only person and thing I really had was my mom and my teddy bear. But I cannot go to my mom and tell her my problem's and how I felt because I wanted to be a man, and when I say that I look at my grandpa and see how he was a man so when he died that is what I tried to do. He never went to someone for his problems, he always kept it to himself and figured it out. So, I figure It out just like he did and that is me imaging me seeing my teddy bear. Do not get me wrong, I know this sounds childish but just hear me out. When I say I imagine seeing my teddy bear is not the fact that I am seeing it, it is the fact that it brings back memories and joy that I had as a child. Me being adle to do that it made me realize that it is ok to be alone and its part of growing up. But I also realized that I had to put the teddy bear down and really learn how to grow as a person. I used to always hug my teddy bear when I was sad, alone, etc. But the more that I think about being alone and how sometimes I get alone being across the country from my family, friends, and girlfriend. It came to me that it is fully okay to be alone and I courage some people to not be afraid of it because it taught me so much about life and how much we should appreciate it. One thing that it taught me was you need to be able to let go for your past and your past mistakes because when you are alone and you get into your brain about everything your start to think back to things you wish you could of changed in order to be happy or not alone anymore. The thing is in life you are always going to make mistakes, there is no denying that because we are all human, but it is what you do after those mistakes that really define you as a person. Another thing that it was taught me was that I really found out the person I want to be, and it made me mature into a better person. When I say it made me mature into a better person I mean I start to appreciate the little things in life and made me appreciate the people that I have so any second, I have time to hang out with them or just to see them I made it happen or I sacrifice something that doesn't mean as much. I really experienced this when I went home for thanksgiving. I was so so happy to see my family, friends, and my gf again because I was always alone here at Pitt and, honestly, I am grateful for it and the lesson it has taught me. That is why I can say that if you really wanna grow as a person you must learn to be by yourself because you are not always goanna have someone there for you or by your side and when you do not you must learn how deal and coupe with it. I am goanna leave you with one quote that always stuck with me and its by Yousuf Karsh and its states “I have also seen that great men are often lonely. This is understandable, because they have Bulit such high standards for themselves that they often feel alone. But that same loneliness is part of their ability to create.” I like this quote so much because instead of being just a sad thing, this loneliness helps you be creative and produce new ideas. Being alone gives you the time and space to think deeply and focus on your work without distractions. So, while loneliness might seem tough, it helps you achieve great things.
